info.title: Kolm API
info.description: HTTP contract for the kolm compiler. Self-hosted (your own kolm server) and kolm.ai (managed) speak the exact same /v1 surface. JSON in, JSON out, bearer or X-Kolm-Key auth. Every successful run produces an HMAC-signed receipt over (cid, input, output, ts, k_score).

get.description: Public /health – no provider-key leakage. Authenticated callers can hit /v1/health for the full snapshot including backend availability.

get.description: authMiddleware so a Prometheus scraper can reach it without an API key, optionally gated by KOLM_METRICS_BEARER for prod deployments. The honest-dev-default is public; setting the env var requires `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and returns a structured 401 envelope on mismatch so misconfigured scrapers fail loud.

post.summary: Self-serve cancel / downgrade-to-free. Any paid tenant can drop to the
post.description: Self-serve cancel / downgrade-to-free. Any paid tenant can drop to the free tier without contacting anyone.

post.summary: Self-serve plan changes. Free is applied instantly (this is also the
post.description: Self-serve plan changes. Free is applied instantly (this is also the downgrade / cancel path). Paid plans return a Stripe Payment Link with `client_reference_id=<tenant_id>`; the plan is flipped only when the webhook receives `checkout.session.completed`. This guarantees the tenant cannot get paid features without paying.

get.summary: can read end-to-end. Tenant metadata + signed receipts + audit log +
get.description: can read end-to-end. Tenant metadata + signed receipts + audit log + BAA-ready org details + control mapping snapshot. JSON only (no PHI ever transits this endpoint – receipts are payload-free by design). Auditors who want a literal ZIP can pipe `curl ... | jq -r .b64_zip | base64 -d`, but the JSON shape is the canonical artifact.

post.summary: Self-serve account delete. Soft-delete the tenant; receipts and artifacts
post.description: Self-serve account delete. Soft-delete the tenant; receipts and artifacts already shipped to users keep verifying since the cloud signing key is unchanged. The tenant can no longer authenticate. If they had an active Stripe subscription we ask Stripe to cancel it so they aren't charged again – best-effort, never blocks deletion.

get.summary: Self-serve data export (/privacy promises this). Returns a JSON bundle
get.description: Self-serve data export (/privacy promises this). Returns a JSON bundle of the tenant's row plus every concept, compile job, observation, and invocation we have on file for them. We strip secrets (api_key_hash, stripe_customer_id keeps existing for portability, billing tokens drop). Content-Disposition nudges browsers to save the file.

post.summary: an HTTP response would balloon memory, leak the server's checkout layout,
post.description: an HTTP response would balloon memory, leak the server's checkout layout, and lock the route into one host's filesystem. The CLI is the supported path – `kolm artifact airgap --out <path>`. The route still exists so surface discovery + docs can link to it and so a future CDN-backed implementation has a stable URL.

post.summary: Attestation callback – UNAUTH (no API key). Identifies via enroll_token,
post.description: Attestation callback – UNAUTH (no API key). Identifies via enroll_token, which was minted by the deploy endpoint and embedded in the deploy script. The token is single-use-equivalent: if an attacker has the token they could spoof the public_url, but the deployment row is owned by a specific tenant_id and the URL is visible in their dashboard – they'll notice.

post.summary: Customer deploys a .kolm artifact to their own Fly / AWS Nitro / GCP CVM
post.description: Customer deploys a .kolm artifact to their own Fly / AWS Nitro / GCP CVM / Azure CVM / Docker host. We issue a signed deploy manifest + a deploy script the customer runs. The deployed instance POSTs an attestation (image SHA, plus TEE measurement on confidential targets) back to /v1/byoc/attestation. kolm.ai never runs the artifact.

post.description: Verified-inference endpoint – Generator-Verifier asymmetry made shippable. Sample k candidates from a frontier model, run each through a deterministic Recipe verifier (test cases), pick the first that passes. Returns a receipt. P(correct) >= 1 - (1 - p*v)^k. For p=0.91 v=1 k=8: 99.9999%. Uses the server's ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, so a tenant API key is required.

post.summary: Verify - compiles caller-supplied source and runs the verify suite against
post.description: Verify - compiles caller-supplied source and runs the verify suite against positive/negative examples. Returns the verify result block (per-case pass/fail + summary). Rate-limited via publishLimiter. Body: { source (required), positives, negatives }.

Used by the kolm-audit-1 `verify_url` field; the third-party can hit this without auth to get back the signed payload + a deterministic verify result.

get.description: /v1/whoami alias. SDKs (OpenAI/Anthropic/LangChain) follow the convention `GET /v1/whoami` (or `/v1/me`) for "who is this key?". apiKeyAuth.name: X-Kolm-Key
apiKeyAuth.description: Long-lived API key. Read from /v1/account. Use this for CI.

compiles_remaining.description: Compiles left before quota or hard cap.

recipe.description: Free-form recipe object: task, objective, adapter, evaluator. See /docs/manifest-v0.1.json for the canonical shape.

examples.description: Optional in-the-loop examples. Each is {input, expected}.

base_model.description: Base model identifier. Defaults are picked by the compile planner if omitted.

k_min.description: Hard floor for this artifact's K-score. Compile fails closed if the artifact's measured K (against its own eval set) is below this. Per-artifact, not per-model.

compliance_pack.description: Optional pre-baked compliance pack id (HIPAA, SR 11-7, NIST AI RMF, etc.).

k_score.description: This artifact's K-score (per-.kolm, not per-base-model); null until status=done.

artifact_url.description: Signed pre-fetch URL for the .kolm. Null until status=done.
Either artifact_cid OR artifact_url, not both.

input.description: Free-form. String for chat, object for structured tasks.

output.description: Free-form. Shape mirrors the artifact's declared output schema.

Receipt.description: HMAC-signed receipt over (cid, input_hash, output_hash, ts).

Manifest.description: Canonical kolm manifest. CID is sha256 of the canonical-JSON serialization of {hashes}.

hashes.description: Map of {entry_path: sha256:...} for every file in the .kolm zip.

JsonEnvelope.description: Canonical envelope. Successful responses set ok=true; failures carry ok=false plus error/hint.

GenericRequest.description: Permissive request body envelope used for auto-generated route shells. Curated endpoints override this with a richer per-endpoint schema. additionalProperties is true so callers can pass forward-compatible fields without breaking the contract.

example.hint: set Authorization: Bearer <kolm_api_key> (ks_*/kao_*) or X-API-Key header

NotFound.description: No record for the given id or CID.

example.hint: check the request body / required fields

JsonEnvelope.description: Standard {ok, ...} envelope. Successful 2xx responses share this shape unless overridden.

ServerError.description: 500 — unexpected upstream error. The envelope describes the failure mode.

example.hint: capture the error_id and include it in any bug report
